page: Microsoft Azure E20d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 10 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 160.0 GB of system memory, which provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance includes 768 GB of local NVMe storage.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most compute tasks, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1899.88 and a multi-core score of 19418.85. However, memory performance is a notable strength, with a Passmark memory score of 2801.06, placing it in the top 25% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth reaches 1042.6 GB/sec for cached reads and 112.59 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

While the server excels in memory-intensive operations, it exhibits clear limitations in heavy compute workloads such as large-scale artificial intelligence. LLM inference for a large 70B model is poor, registering at only 4.19 tokens per second. Consequently, this server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and data processing tasks rather than large-model AI inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E20d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 20 dedicated vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU, complemented by 768 GB of local storage. Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 25% and cached read bandwidth of 1042.6 GB/sec. Conversely, compute-heavy tasks like large language model inference represent a performance tradeoff, with 70B model processing scoring in the bottom 10%. The instance is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160 GB of RAM, and 768 GB of local storage, designed for database and memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20d_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 20 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 768 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and local storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 20 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
160.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio
768 GB of integrated local storage for fast data access
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Suited for databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications
Poorly suited for large-scale LLM inference and heavy AI workloads
